quiz.title: Unit 8 Quiz 1, Biology I
quiz.subtitle: Pathogens: Types, Characteristics, and Disease Examples
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Pathogen Definition, Bacteria, Viruses, Fungi, Protozoa, Helminths, Disease Transmission, Antibiotics

questions.question: A pathogen is best defined as:
questions.explanation: A pathogen is any organism or agent that causes disease. This includes bacteria, viruses, fungi, protozoa, and helminths. Harmless microorganisms are not pathogens, and immune cells/antibodies fight pathogens rather than causing disease.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT considered a pathogen?
questions.explanation: Bread yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) is not typically pathogenic and is used in baking. While some fungi are pathogens, bread yeast is generally harmless. Bacteria, viruses, and protozoa can all be pathogens.

questions.question: Which pathogen is made of genetic material enclosed in a protein coat?
questions.explanation: Viruses consist of genetic material (DNA or RNA) surrounded by a protein coat called a capsid. Some viruses also have an envelope. Bacteria, fungi, and protozoa are cellular organisms with more complex structures.

questions.question: Viruses differ from other pathogens because they:
options: Require a host cell to reproduce
Are visible under a light microscope
questions.explanation: Viruses are obligate intracellular parasites that require a host cell's machinery to reproduce. They are acellular and cannot replicate on their own. Most viruses are too small to be seen with light microscopes.

questions.question: Which type of pathogen is prokaryotic?
questions.explanation: Bacteria are prokaryotic organisms, meaning they lack a membrane-bound nucleus and organelles. Fungi and protozoa are eukaryotes, while viruses are acellular and not classified as prokaryotic or eukaryotic.

questions.question: Which pathogen causes diseases such as tuberculosis and cholera?
questions.explanation: Tuberculosis is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis and cholera by Vibrio cholerae, both bacteria. Viruses cause influenza, fungi cause athlete's foot, and helminths cause parasitic worm infections.

questions.question: Fungi that cause disease are usually:
questions.explanation: Fungi are eukaryotic organisms with membrane-bound organelles. They are not photosynthetic (unlike plants), not prokaryotic (unlike bacteria), and not viruses. Pathogenic fungi include Candida and Aspergillus.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases is caused by a fungus?
questions.explanation: Athlete's foot (tinea pedis) is caused by a fungus (dermatophyte). Influenza is viral, malaria is protozoan, and cholera is bacterial.

questions.question: Protozoan pathogens are best described as:
questions.explanation: Protozoa are single-celled eukaryotic organisms. They are not multicellular (helminths are), not acellular (viruses are), and not prokaryotic (bacteria are). Examples include Plasmodium (malaria) and Entamoeba.

questions.question: Which pathogen is typically transmitted by a vector such as a mosquito?
questions.explanation: Protozoan pathogens like Plasmodium (malaria) are often transmitted by mosquito vectors. Some viruses (dengue, Zika) are also vector-borne, but protozoa are classic examples. Fungi and bacteria are less commonly transmitted by vectors.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a helminth?
questions.explanation: Roundworms (nematodes) are helminths - parasitic worms. Bacteria, viruses, and yeast (fungus) are not classified as helminths.

questions.question: Helminths are best described as:
questions.explanation: Helminths are parasitic worms, including flatworms (tapeworms) and roundworms. They are multicellular eukaryotes, not single-celled, not bacteria, and not viruses.

questions.question: Which type of pathogen is the largest in size?
questions.explanation: Helminths (parasitic worms) are the largest pathogens, visible to the naked eye. Viruses are smallest, followed by bacteria, then protozoa. Helminths can be several meters long in some cases.

questions.question: Which pathogen can be treated with antibiotics?
questions.explanation: Antibiotics target bacterial structures like cell walls or protein synthesis. They are ineffective against viruses, fungi (require antifungals), and protozoa (require antiprotozoal drugs).

questions.question: Which structure allows some bacteria to survive harsh conditions?
questions.explanation: Endospores are dormant structures formed by some bacteria (e.g., Bacillus, Clostridium) that allow survival in extreme heat, drought, and chemicals. Capsules aid in virulence, flagella help movement, and ribosomes make proteins.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the different types of pathogens! 🦠
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between bacteria, viruses, and fungi.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on which diseases are caused by which pathogens.
scoreMessages.0: Please review pathogen definitions, transmission methods, and treatments.
